mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50 percent of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.5 percent, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, causing investors to discount equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in greater than 65 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above the 6.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 20-day returns in greater than 55 percent of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation regime where CPI exceeds 230, persistently low federal funds rates create negative real yields and force expectations of aggressive future tightening, triggering short-term equity sell-offs.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 60 percent of high-inflation low-rate observations, or the signal reverses direction when inflation falls below 220.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and corporate revenue growth, which mechanically supports higher S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains negative over consecutive 2-year windows, or positive industrial production growth correlates with negative equity returns in greater than 50 percent of months.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payrolls additions exceeding 200,000 signal robust labor market expansion and rising household income, which fuels consumer spending and drives positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative in greater than 60 percent of months where payrolls exceed the 200,000 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent high GDP growth and elevated inflation indicate an overheating economy that forces monetary authorities to tighten policy, compressing equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The combined high-growth high-inflation signal produces positive 60-day returns in greater than 55 percent of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recovery phases, a declining unemployment rate alongside rising industrial production confirms synchronized economic expansion, generating strong positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The signal yields negative 20-day returns in greater than 50 percent of recovery-phase observations, or the predictive power vanishes when unemployment remains above 5.0 percent.
